title: Command for sending regular payments for a recurring BOLT12 offer
description: The **repeatpay** RPC command schedules repeated payments as indicated by an (experimental!) recurrence feature in an BOLT12 offer.
The command will arrange payments on the schedule indicated the offer until one of:
1. The recurrence expires.  Some invoices only specify a limited number of repetitions.
2. The **cancelrepeatpay** command causes the payment to cease.

3. A payment is missed.  This could be because of a local error (such as unavailable currency conversion, or we were offline), the node was unreachable for fetching an invoice, the node gave an invalid response, insufficient fuds, xpay failure, or the amount requested was too much.  **repeatpay** will attempt to make payments multiple times leading up to the deadline.
Note that it is perfectly possible to have multiple repeatpay commands for the same bolt12 offer.  The `payer_id` assigned to each will be different.
A bolt12 offer which contains a (draft!) recurrence specification.

*maxamount* is the maximum amount to authorize per payment.  It is either in millisatoshi precision; it can be a whole number, or a whole number with suffix *msat* or *sat*, or a three decimal point number with suffix *sat*, or an 1 to 11 decimal point number suffixed by *btc*, or a number with an ISO 4217 postfix (e.g. USD), in which case currency conversion will need to be done for the invoice itself. A plugin is needed which provides the `currencyconvert` API for this currency, otherwise the repeatpay creation will fail.
Unique label to identify this recurring payment
Which period index to start paying from (default 0).
The offer we are paying (same as the bolt12 parameter).
The maximum millisatoshi amount we are authorized to pay per payment for the first invoice (it may change with each invoice, as we do further currency conversion).
The original currency amount specified by the user (e.g. '100.00USD'), if a currency was given.
Unique label to identify this recurring payment
The transient id we have used to identify ourselves as a (repeat) payer.
The random data we use to derive our payer_id.
Current status of the recurring payment.
Number of payments successfully completed so far.
Log of status transitions and notable events.

author: Rusty Russell [rusty@rustcorp.com.au](mailto:rusty@rustcorp.com.au) is mainly responsible.
